title: ctenopteris pensilis
intro: Ctenopteris pensilis, also known as the Australian Water Fern, is a type of aquatic fern native to Australia and New Zealand. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating wounds, infections, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Ctenopteris pensilis include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing, as well as its potential to alleviate symptoms of eczema and other skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Ctenopteris pensilis can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, and are often used topically to apply the plant's medicinal properties directly to the skin.
